mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate weakening aggregate demand and rising household financial stress, which jointly compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a sustained increase in the industrial production index demonstrates supply-side resilience that supports corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in high-inflation periods do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 0.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business confidence and consumer spending momentum, which directly boosts equity market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Spearman rank correlation between HOUST changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% for two consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance suppresses corporate valuation multiples and equity risk premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over the 60-day window in over 65% of triggered episodes, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising unemployment and declining non-farm payrolls indicate a structural labor market breakdown that erodes consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-growth economic environment, a sequential rise in real GDP coupled with stable housing starts signals robust corporate revenue growth, which directly fuels positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not significantly outperform the baseline in high-growth quarters, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.
